skill.name: monorepo-management
skill.description: Guide monorepo setup, migration, dependency management, build optimization, and CI workflows using Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm workspaces.
skill.summary: Plan and improve scalable JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples for monorepo setup, package scripts, CI workflows, and configuration files. No evidence found of executable payloads, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Runtime Execution
low_findings.description: Static matches are fenced examples for npx, pnpm, nx, turbo, and shell-based CI commands. The skill contains guidance text and examples, not code that executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is inside Markdown code fences or inline resource references. No executable script file, command runner, or dynamic user-input execution path is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Configuration File References Are Standard Monorepo Examples
low_findings.description: References to .env patterns, .npmrc, ~/.pnpm-store, and .eslintrc are configuration examples. They do not read local secrets or instruct unauthorized file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden-file references appear in configuration snippets for Turborepo, pnpm, and ESLint. There is no evidence of reading or transmitting file contents.

low_findings.title: GitHub Actions Secret Names Are Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The workflow example names GITHUB_TOKEN and NPM_TOKEN as GitHub Actions secrets for release publishing. No token value is hardcoded and no external destination receives the secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token names are used in a standard CI env block for changesets publishing. This remains worth human awareness, but the snippet does not expose actual credentials.

low_findings.title: Schema URL Is Benign Documentation Metadata
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the public Turborepo JSON schema inside a configuration example. It is not a network request made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a schema identifier inside a turbo.json example. No fetch, curl, webhook, or data transfer operation is present at this location.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Has No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography flag points to the skill description. That line describes monorepo management and does not reference cryptography or hashing algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only descriptive marketplace text. No MD5, SHA1, cipher configuration, or cryptographic API appears there.

content.value_statement: Large JavaScript and TypeScript repositories can become slow and hard to coordinate. This skill helps plan workspace structure, shared packages, caching, and CI workflows with Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm.
actual_capabilities: Explains when a monorepo is useful and which tradeoffs to expect.
Provides setup patterns for Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm workspaces.
Shows package structure patterns for shared UI, utilities, types, and configuration.
Guides build, lint, test, and type-check task orchestration across packages.
Includes CI examples for GitHub Actions and affected-project deployment.
Covers publishing workflows using changesets and workspace package scripts.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not execute commands by itself.
Examples focus on JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos.
Tool versions in examples may need updates for a specific project.
It does not replace repository-specific security, access, or release review.

use_cases.title: Start a New Product Monorepo
use_cases.description: Design apps, shared packages, workspace scripts, and build cache settings before creating a new repository.

use_cases.title: Migrate Repositories Into One Workspace
use_cases.description: Plan a migration from separate repositories to a shared monorepo with clear dependencies and release boundaries.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow CI Pipelines
use_cases.description: Identify better task filtering, caching, affected-project builds, and package-level validation for faster checks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Monorepo Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm workspaces for a JavaScript monorepo with these apps and packages: [describe projects].

prompt_templates.title: Design Workspace Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Propose a monorepo folder structure for [apps] and [shared packages]. Include workspace scripts, package boundaries, and naming conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Build and Test Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this monorepo task setup and suggest improvements for caching, affected builds, linting, testing, and type checking: [paste summary].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased migration plan from multiple repositories into a monorepo. Cover dependency mapping, CI changes, package publishing, and rollback risks.

output_examples.input: A team asks how to structure a web app, docs site, shared UI package, and shared TypeScript config.
output_examples.output: The response recommends apps for deployable projects, packages for shared code, workspace scripts at the root, and separate package ownership boundaries.

output_examples.input: A repository has slow CI because every pull request builds all packages.
output_examples.output: The response suggests affected-project filtering, explicit cache inputs and outputs, separate lint and type-check tasks, and deployment only for changed apps.

output_examples.input: A team wants to publish internal packages from a monorepo.
output_examples.output: The response outlines changeset-based versioning, release pull requests, package visibility decisions, and token handling through CI secrets.

best_practices: Define clear ownership and dependency boundaries before adding shared packages.
Configure cache inputs and outputs explicitly for build, test, lint, and type-check tasks.
Keep secrets in CI secret stores and review release workflows before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not move unrelated projects into one repository without shared tooling or shared release needs.
Do not allow packages to rely on undeclared dependencies from the root workspace.
Do not cache build outputs without tracking every input that can change the result.

faq.question: Does this skill execute monorepo commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides examples and guidance. The user or agent must decide whether to run any command.

faq.question: Which tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Turborepo, Nx, pnpm workspaces, shared package patterns, GitHub Actions, and changesets.

faq.question: Is it only for frontend projects?
faq.answer: The examples focus on JavaScript and TypeScript, but many planning ideas also apply to backend packages.

faq.question: Can it help with CI performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains affected builds, task filtering, cache configuration, and deploy-only-changed workflows.

faq.question: Can it design a migration from many repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help plan phases, package boundaries, dependency cleanup, CI changes, and release strategy.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It includes basic CI secret handling in examples, but repository security still needs separate review.
